questions.question: A hiking trail has a slope of $2$. What is the slope of a perpendicular trail?
If a line has a slope of $-3$ and passes through $(1, 2)$, what is the equation of a perpendicular line passing through $(1, 2)$?
A fuel consumption study found that the rate of fuel consumption is $-2$ gallons per mile. What is the slope of a line perpendicular to this rate?
A animal population study modeled the population growth with the equation $y = -4x + 10$. What is the equation of a line perpendicular to this model?
A line has a slope of $-\frac{3}{4}$ and passes through $(2, 5)$. What is the slope of a line perpendicular to this line?
If two lines are perpendicular, and one line has a slope of $5$, what is the slope of the other line?
A line passes through the points $(1, 2)$ and $(3, 4)$. What is the slope of a line perpendicular to this line?
The equation of a line is $y = 2x - 5$. What is the equation of a line perpendicular to this line and passing through $(0, 0)$?

A park ranger is designing a new hiking trail. The trail will have a slope of $-2$ and will pass through the point $(3, 4)$. Find the equation of a line that is perpendicular to the trail and passes through the point $(3, 4)$. Show all work and explain your reasoning.

A wildlife biologist is studying the population of a certain species of animal. The population is modeled by the equation $y = -3x + 12$. Find the equation of a line that is perpendicular to this model and passes through the point $(2, 6)$. Show all work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Make sure to read each question carefully and understand what is being asked.
Use the negative reciprocal to find the slope of a perpendicular line.
Show all work and explain your reasoning for free response questions.
